---
title: "Lecture 9: CSS Units & Advanced Selectors"
subtitle: "{{< meta course.number >}} - {{< meta course.title >}}"
author: "{{< meta instructor.name >}}"
date: "February 9, 2026"
code-annotations: below
---


## {#title-slide data-menu-title="Title Slide" background="#053660"}

<div class="custom-title">
[Lecture 9]{.baby-blue-text}
</div>

<div class="custom-subtitle">
[CSS Units & Advanced Selectors]{.white-text}
</div>

<div class="custom-subtitle3">
Week 3 | Unit II: CSS Styling & JavaScript<br>
{{< meta course.number >}} - {{< meta course.title >}}<br>
{{< meta instructor.name >}}, {{< meta instructor.title >}}
</div>

---

## {data-menu-title="Learning Objectives"}

[Learning Objectives]{.slide-title}

<hr>

::: {.incremental .body-text-m}
- Understand absolute vs relative CSS units
- Master responsive sizing with relative units
- Write advanced CSS selectors
- Combine selectors for precise targeting
- Optimize CSS for readability and maintainability
:::

::: {.notes}
CSS units are fundamental for responsive design. Understanding both absolute and relative units is crucial for modern web development.
:::

---

## {data-menu-title="Absolute Units"}

[Absolute CSS Units]{.slide-title}

<hr>

Fixed size units (don't change):

```css
body { font-size: 16px; }      /* pixels */
h1 { font-size: 96pt; }        /* points (print) */
p { font-size: 12mm; }         /* millimeters (print) */
div { width: 10cm; }           /* centimeters (print) */
```

::: {.body-text-m .topbr}
**Best for:**
- Print stylesheets
- Pixel-perfect designs
- Fixed layouts (not recommended anymore)
:::

---

## {data-menu-title="Relative Units"}

[Relative CSS Units (Recommended)]{.slide-title}

<hr>

::: columns
::: {.column width="50%"}
[**em & rem**]{.teal-text}

```css
p { font-size: 1em; }
/* 1em = current font size */

h1 { font-size: 2rem; }
/* 1rem = root (html) size */
```

Perfect for scaling
:::

::: {.column width="50%"}
[**Percentages**]{.teal-text}

```css
width: 100%;
/* 100% of parent */

height: 50%;
/* 50% of parent */
```

Great for responsive layouts
:::
:::

---

## {data-menu-title="Viewport Units"}

[Viewport-based Units]{.slide-title2}

<hr>

Units based on screen size:

```css
width: 100vw;        /* 100% of viewport width */
height: 100vh;       /* 100% of viewport height */
font-size: 5vw;      /* 5% of viewport width */
padding: 2vh;        /* 2% of viewport height */
```

::: {.fragment .body-text-m .topbr}
**Perfect for:**
- Full-screen sections
- Responsive typography
- Hero images
:::

---

## {data-menu-title="Comparing Units"}

[Unit Comparison Chart]{.slide-title}

<hr>

```
px (pixels)     → Fixed size, precise
em             → Relative to parent element
rem            → Relative to root (html)
%              → Relative to parent
vw/vh          → Relative to viewport
```

**Example:**
```html
<html> <!-- root = 16px -->
  <body style="font-size: 20px;">
    <p style="font-size: 1em;">
      This is 20px (1em = parent)
    </p>
    <p style="font-size: 1rem;">
      This is 16px (1rem = root)
    </p>
  </body>
</html>
```

---

## {data-menu-title="Type Selectors"}

[Type (Element) Selectors]{.slide-title}

<hr>

Select all elements of a type:

```{.css code-line-numbers="|1|2|3"}
p { color: blue; }          /* All paragraphs */
h1 { font-size: 32px; }     /* All h1 headings */
body { background: white; } /* Body element */
```

**Group selectors:**
```css
h1, h2, h3 {
    color: #333;
    font-weight: bold;
}
/* Applies to all h1, h2, and h3 */
```

---

## {data-menu-title="Class & ID Selectors"}

[Class and ID Selectors]{.slide-title2}

<hr>

```{.css code-line-numbers="|1-3|5-6"}
/* Class selector (multiple use) */
.highlight {
    background-color: yellow;
}

/* ID selector (unique) */
#header {
    background-color: navy;
    color: white;
}
```

**In HTML:**
```html
<p class="highlight">Highlighted text</p>
<div id="header">Page Header</div>
```

---

## {data-menu-title="Descendant Selectors"}

[Descendant & Child Selectors]{.slide-title}

<hr>

**Descendant selector** (any nested element):

```{.css code-line-numbers="|1"}
article p { color: #666; }
/* All <p> inside <article>, no matter how deep */
```

**Child selector** (direct children only):

```{.css code-line-numbers="|1"}
article > p { color: blue; }
/* Only <p> that are direct children of <article> */
```

**Sibling selector:**

```css
h1 + p { font-weight: bold; }
/* <p> that comes right after <h1> */
```

---

## {data-menu-title="Attribute Selectors"}

[Attribute Selectors]{.slide-title}

<hr>

Select by HTML attributes:

```{.css code-line-numbers="|1|2|3|4|5"}
input[type="text"] { border: 1px solid blue; }
a[target="_blank"] { color: red; }
img[alt] { opacity: 0.9; }
a[href^="https"] { color: green; } /* Starts with */
a[href$=".pdf"] { color: orange; }  /* Ends with */
```

::: {.fragment .body-text-m .center-text .topbr}
[Very powerful for styling specific elements!]{.magenta-text}
:::

---

## {data-menu-title="Pseudo-classes"}

[Pseudo-classes]{.slide-title}

<hr>

Special selector states:

```{.css code-line-numbers="|1|2|3|4|5"}
a:hover { color: red; }           /* On mouse hover */
input:focus { border: 2px blue; } /* When focused */
p:first-child { font-weight: bold; } /* First child */
li:last-child { margin-bottom: 0; }  /* Last child */
p:nth-child(2n) { background: #f0f0f0; } /* Every 2nd */
```

---

## {data-menu-title="Pseudo-elements"}

[Pseudo-elements]{.slide-title}

<hr>

Create virtual elements:

```{.css code-line-numbers="|1|2|3|4"}
p::first-letter { font-size: 2em; } /* First letter */
p::first-line { font-weight: bold; } /* First line */
a::before { content: "[Link] "; }  /* Before content */
a::after { content: " →"; }        /* After content */
```

**Common use:**
```css
/* Add decorative content */
li::before {
    content: "→ ";
    color: blue;
}
```

---

## {data-menu-title="Specificity"}

[CSS Specificity & Cascade]{.slide-title}

<hr>

How CSS decides which rule wins:

```
ID selectors        → 100 points  (#header)
Class selectors     → 10 points   (.highlight)
Element selectors   → 1 point     (p, h1)
```

**Example:**
```css
/* 1 point */
p { color: blue; }

/* 10 points - wins! */
.highlight { color: red; }

/* 100 points - always wins */
#main { color: green; }
```

::: {.fragment .body-text-s .center-text .topbr}
Tip: Avoid `!important` - use specificity instead
:::

---

## {data-menu-title="Combining Selectors"}

[Combining Selectors for Power]{.slide-title}

<hr>

```{.css code-line-numbers="|1|2|3|4"}
/* Descendant */
.container p { color: blue; }

/* Child */
.container > p { font-weight: bold; }

/* Multiple classes */
.container.active { background: yellow; }

/* Attribute + class */
input[type="text"].error { border: red; }
```

---

## {data-menu-title="Practical Example"}

[Real-world Selector Example]{.slide-title}

<hr>

```{.css code-line-numbers="|1-3|5-7|9-11|13-15"}
/* Style all links */
a { color: #0066cc; }

/* External links differently */
a[target="_blank"] { color: #ff9900; }

/* Links in navigation */
nav a { text-decoration: none; }

/* Links when user hovers */
nav a:hover { text-decoration: underline; }

/* First link in navigation */
nav a:first-child { font-weight: bold; }
```

---

## {data-menu-title="Resources"}

[Resources & References]{.slide-title}

<hr>

::: {.body-text-m}
- [MDN: CSS Units](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Learn/CSS/Building_blocks/Values_and_units)
- [MDN: Selectors](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/CSS/CSS_Selectors)
- [CSS Specificity Calculator](https://specificity.keegan.st/)
- [Course content: Week 3](/content/week-03.qmd)
:::

---

## {#end-slide data-menu-title="End Slide" background="#053660"}

<div class="page-center">
<div class="custom-subtitle">
[Questions?]{.white-text}
</div>
<div class="custom-subtitle3">
Next: Lecture 10 - CSS Box Model & Positioning
</div>
</div>
